refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model
Batch update mechanism to replace the entire worksite model at once
Event-driven update trigger to initiate model updates upon relevant changes
Scheduled periodic update trigger to refresh the model at fixed intervals
Parallel processing of update data to reduce latency
Prioritization and filtering of update data to focus on critical changes only
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model

potential_sources: Tight coupling between update trigger and update mechanism
Dependency of update mechanism on data source formats
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP supports independence by focusing on incremental updates, which can be triggered by various mechanisms (event-driven or scheduled) without tightly coupling the update logic to the trigger. This separation helps maintain modularity and supports meeting latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that incremental changes can be reliably detected and applied within the latency target
Risk that complex incremental logic may introduce errors or inconsistencies in the model
Assumes availability of timely and accurate data to support incremental updates
Potential risk if update triggers are not well synchronized with data changes, causing stale or delayed updates
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the update trigger mechanism (event-driven vs scheduled) as a separate DP to further decouple triggering from update processing and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing and updating the worksite model, including applying incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and status data from the Vehicle and sends updated model state information.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and update instructions from Teleoperation or external control systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems for synchronization and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental update mechanism logically belongs here; minor diffusion due to necessary communication with Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical actuation and sensing; incremental update logic would be scattered or duplicated.
Partial containment as Teleoperation issues commands, but update mechanism is not its core responsibility.
Poor containment; Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to model update logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes worksite model management and minimizes diffusion by interfacing with Vehicle and Teleoperation through defined interfaces.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of update logic.
If interfaces are not well defined, update mechanism may spread into other modules, reducing cohesion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and manageable diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by applying only changes, which supports efficient and timely updates as per the acceptance criteria (p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s).

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of update logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation are clearly defined, minimizing diffusion risk and maintaining cohesion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible since the NGHE Solution module encompasses the core logic responsible for the update mechanism and interfaces with external systems to receive data and commands, enabling measurement of update latency and correctness.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and unambiguous, with well-defined interfaces supporting the realization of the incremental update mechanism.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and manageable diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no recommended revisions. The incremental update mechanism DP directly addresses the FR with acceptable latency, and the IM allocation is clear with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition ensures clear containment and verification feasibility, reducing complexity and risk. Therefore, further decomposition is not necessary at this stage.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the timeliness and correctness requirements as specified.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules remain stable and well-defined.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of update logic, but is currently manageable.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating update latency ≤ 1.0 s under operational conditions.
Interface stability and integration test results with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
